frameworks.name: fMRI repetition suppression
frameworks.one_line: Reduced BOLD on repeated stimuli used to index neural population coding.

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: Alternative fMRI meso-scale technique briefly contrasted with repetition suppression.

core_question: Can fMRI repetition suppression validly index neural representations at the population (meso-scale) level?
key_assumptions: BOLD signal can be refined to reflect sub-voxel neural population activity
Repetition of a stimulus attribute reveals the neural code for that attribute
key_findings: Repetition suppression and MVPA have emerged as the main tools to probe meso-scale neural coding in human fMRI
interpretations: Repetition suppression can investigate how representations are encoded across populations and transformed by computations, but has physiological limitations
limitations_noted: Physiological basis and limitations of fMRI repetition suppression constrain its interpretation
